questions.correct: All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space
questions.questionText: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
wrong: OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
It eliminates context switching overhead entirely
It runs exclusively on virtual machines

questions.correct: To provide an interface for user programs to request services from the kernel
questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of a system call?
wrong: To compile source code files into machine code
To clear CPU cache configurations automatically
To manage graphical window rendering pipelines

questions.correct: Higher system stability and isolation of corrupted drivers
questions.questionText: What is a major advantage of a microkernel compared to a monolithic design?
wrong: Faster execution performance speeds
Smaller total storage size footprint
Lower context switching overhead costs

questions.correct: Kernel mode (Ring 0)
questions.questionText: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
wrong: User mode (Ring 3)
Hypervisor mode
Safe mode

questions.correct: The CPU suspends its execution stream to run an interrupt service routine
questions.questionText: What happens during a hardware interrupt?
wrong: The operating system completely reboots its kernel
The active process is terminated and marked as a zombie
Virtual memory maps flush physical hard drive tracks

questions.correct: Round Robin (RR)
questions.questionText: Which scheduling algorithm runs processes based on a fixed time-slice quantum rotation?
wrong: Shortest Job First (SJF)
First-Come, First-Served (FCFS)
Multilevel Feedback Queue

questions.correct: Long-running processes can suffer from starvation
questions.questionText: What is a major disadvantage of the Shortest Job First scheduling algorithm?
wrong: High context-switching processing overhead
Massive memory cache utilization footprints
It requires specialized graphical hardware layers

questions.correct: Process state, program counter, CPU registers, and memory boundaries
questions.questionText: What information is tracked inside a Process Control Block (PCB)?
wrong: Local database storage tables metadata
The user web browser cache profiles
Network interface card MAC address parameters

questions.correct: A process has its own isolated address space; threads share address spaces
questions.questionText: What differentiates a process from a thread?
wrong: Threads run in kernel mode; processes run in user mode
Processes share file descriptors by default; threads do not
A single thread can contain multiple independent processes

questions.correct: Blocked / Waiting state
questions.questionText: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
wrong: Ready state
Running state
Zombie state

questions.correct: To map virtual addresses generated by the CPU to physical memory locations
questions.questionText: What is the function of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
wrong: To partition hard drive sectors dynamically at runtime
To allocate network sockets to background daemons
To clear cache registers when a context switch occurs

questions.correct: An interrupt triggered when a program accesses a page not currently mapped in RAM
questions.questionText: What is a page fault?
wrong: A physical hardware failure inside RAM modules
A crash caused by syntax compile errors
A data anomaly where two files share the same sector

questions.correct: Thrashing
questions.questionText: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
wrong: Paging
Fragmenting
Starvation

questions.correct: Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
questions.questionText: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
wrong: External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space

questions.correct: To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
questions.questionText: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
wrong: To handle network data package buffering streams
To store execution metrics logs for background tasks
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk

questions.correct: File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name
questions.questionText: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
wrong: The literal plain-text contents of the target file
The human-readable file name and directory path string
The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions

questions.correct: It records changes in a log to prevent corruption during sudden power losses
questions.questionText: What is a major advantage of a journaling file system like ext4?
wrong: It speeds up graphics card rendering rates
It encrypts all user credentials automatically
It compresses text files to half their initial disk sector allocations

questions.correct: Directory entries (dentries)
questions.questionText: What component maps file paths to matching inode numbers in a file system directory?
wrong: File allocation tables (FAT)
Master boot records (MBR)
Virtual file layers (VFS)

questions.correct: A Hard Link points directly to the same inode; a Symbolic Link points to a path string
questions.questionText: What structural pattern does a Hard Link follow compared to a Symbolic Link?
wrong: Symbolic links can only reference binary files; hard links handle folders
Hard links delete the original file target when broken; symbolic links do not
Hard links require administrative privileges to execute

questions.correct: To provide an abstract uniform interface for applications to access diverse file systems
questions.questionText: What is the primary role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in a kernel?
wrong: To manage virtual memory swap spaces on physical storage disks
To simulate internal hard drive arrays inside local ram spaces
To buffer incoming network package flows before delivery to sockets

questions.correct: Preemption
questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT one of the Coffman conditions required for a deadlock?
wrong: Mutual Exclusion
Hold and Wait
Circular Wait

questions.correct: Banker's Algorithm
questions.questionText: What classical algorithm can be utilized to safely avoid deadlocks in resource allocation?
wrong: Round Robin Algorithm
Dijkstra's Shortest Path
De Morgan's Optimization

questions.correct: Mutex
questions.questionText: What is a binary semaphore commonly called when used strictly as a locking mechanism?
wrong: Monitor
Condition Variable
Critical Section

questions.correct: Race Condition
questions.questionText: What condition occurs when multiple threads modify shared data concurrently with output depending on execution order?
wrong: Deadlock state
Livelock loop
Starvation anomaly

questions.correct: A block of code that accesses shared resources and must not be concurrently run
questions.questionText: What is a critical section inside multi-threaded application software?
wrong: A critical database constraint tracking primary indexing relationships
A protected kernel execution memory register row
A background process handling system initialization routines
